title: Map an Ecosystem Near You
description: Observe and analyze the ecological system in a place you can access.

steps.title: Choose a local ecosystem
steps.instruction: Pick a local ecosystem you can visit or observe: a park, backyard, urban street, pond, forest edge, beach.
steps.prompt: Describe the ecosystem. What biome is it in? What are the dominant abiotic conditions (soil type, moisture, light, temperature range)?

steps.title: List the producers
steps.instruction: Identify at least 3 plant species (or algae, if aquatic). These are your primary producers -- the base of the food web.
steps.prompt: What plants do you see? Try to identify them by name. Estimate their relative abundance.

steps.title: Trace a food chain
steps.instruction: Find evidence of at least one herbivore and one predator. Trace a food chain from producer to top consumer.
steps.prompt: Write your food chain: Producer → Herbivore → Predator. What trophic levels are present?

steps.title: Spot a human impact
steps.instruction: Look for signs of human impact: pollution, invasive species, habitat fragmentation, noise, altered water flow.
steps.prompt: Describe one human impact you observe. How is it affecting the ecosystem?

steps.title: Identify an ecosystem service
steps.instruction: What does this ecosystem do for the surrounding area? Air purification? Flood control? Carbon storage? Recreation?
steps.prompt: Name at least two ecosystem services provided by this location. Would anyone notice if they were lost?

steps.title: Suggest one improvement
steps.instruction: If you could make one change to improve the health of this ecosystem, what would it be?
steps.prompt: Describe your proposed change and explain the ecological reasoning behind it.
